tools.name: yamllint
tools.scope: full project (excludes bin/, .git/, docs/, scripts/, .github/, .claude/, charts/)

tools.name: helm lint
tools.run_command: for chart in $(find charts -name Chart.yaml -exec dirname {} \;); do helm lint "$chart"; done
tools.scope: all helm charts in charts/ directory

linting.notes: All linters passed validation. YAML linter produced one warning about line length (non-blocking). Kube-linter found no issues.

testing.framework: Helm chart snapshot testing + Kustomize build validation
testing.run_single_file: N/A - not applicable for manifest validation
env_vars: GOTOOLCHAIN=auto (required for installing kustomize and yq with Go 1.22)
testing.mock_patterns: Snapshot-based testing - helm template output is compared against golden snapshots
testing.notes: Testing validates that Kustomize manifests build correctly and Helm charts produce expected output. Chart snapshot tests ensure helm template output matches expected snapshots. All 10 chart snapshots passed (7 for odh-rhoai, 3 for rhaii-helm-chart).

gating_checks.name: Best Practices Lint
gating_checks.notes: Uses continue-on-error but still fails workflow if exit code != 0

ci.notes: GitHub Actions workflow testing.yaml runs on pull_request and push to main (excluding .md, LICENSE, .gitignore). Helm.yaml workflow runs only on changes to charts/**. Tekton pipelines appear to be for cluster validation (requires OpenShift cluster). All GitHub Actions checks are gating for PR merges.

build.notes: No traditional 'build' step - this is a GitOps manifest repository. 'make tools' installs validation tools (kustomize, kube-linter, yamllint, yq). Helm must be installed separately. Validation commands verify manifests are correct.

conventions.test_naming: Snapshot files use descriptive names like 'default.snap.yaml', 'all-components-managed.snap.yaml'
conventions.notes: YAML files use 2-space indentation. Kustomize overlays organized in components/, configurations/, dependencies/. Helm charts in charts/ directory. Scripts for automation in scripts/ directory.

lint_commands.notes: Passed with 1 warning about line length (non-blocking)
Not directly validated but covered by ct lint in Helm workflow

test_commands.notes: All 10 snapshot tests passed (7 for odh-rhoai, 3 for rhaii-helm-chart)
Runs all validations: YAML lint, Kustomize build, best practices lint. All passed.

container_recipe.test_single_file: N/A - validation is at kustomization or chart level
container_recipe.notes: Container must have Go >= 1.22 with GOTOOLCHAIN=auto to allow automatic download of newer Go toolchains required by kustomize v5.8.0 and yq v4.49.2. Python3 is required for yamllint. Helm must be installed separately from apt/yum.

results.command: apt-get install -y make curl python3 python3-pip git && curl -fsSL ... | bash (helm install)

validation.summary: All validations passed: YAML lint OK (1 warning), Kustomize build OK (34 kustomizations), best practices lint OK (0 errors), chart tests OK (10 snapshots passed)

gaps: No unit tests - this is a manifest repository, validation is via linting and Kustomize/Helm builds
Tekton pipelines require OpenShift cluster access and cannot be validated locally
No automated integration tests - cluster validation requires actual Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster
Some Makefile targets like 'helm-install-verify' require cluster access and cannot be tested in container
